Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails with a query related to "Greg Pallone" or "Swift" or "cold-chain" (or similar) using search_emails to find Greg's initial email (email_inject_swift_cryo_001) from greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com informing Blessing that the Webb cryogenic equipment requires an LN2-cooled ISO container upgrade adding $2,800 to the original $4,200 quote for a revised total of $7,000, with the earliest pickup April 28 and delivery April 29, and a written approval deadline of April 25. Also find Greg's follow-up email (email_inject_swift_cryo_002) from greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com announcing a cancellation slot allowing Detroit pickup April 30 with May 1 morning delivery at $7,500 total ($7,000 plus $500 rush expedite fee), with confirmation required by end of business April 25 or the slot reverts to the May 8 window which would miss the grant deadline.
OE 2: Search emails with a query related to "Catalina" or "Patricia" or "Webb status" (or similar) using search_emails to find Catalina's forwarded email (email_inject_catalina_fwd_001) from catalina.dubois@moveops.com with subject "FW: Marcus Webb Move: Need Status ASAP" asking Blessing for a status update on both the cold-chain and household goods pieces, referencing Patricia Langford's concern about a lab dock closure and a grant deadline, and stating Catalina needs to get back to Patricia today.
OE 3: Search emails with a query related to "Patricia Langford" or "lab dock" or "Canopy Health" (or similar) using search_emails to find Patricia's original email (email_inject_patricia_webb_001) from patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io (sent to Catalina, CC'd Blessing) confirming the Chicago lab dock is closed April 28 through April 30 for HVAC maintenance and reopens May 1 at 7 AM, and that Marcus Webb has an NIH grant equipment certification deadline on May 2 with $340K in grant funding at risk if the cryogenic equipment is not installed and calibrated before that date.
OE 4: Search emails with a query related to "Lisa Kwan" or "Heartland" or "dispatch hold" (or similar) using search_emails to find Lisa Kwan's email (email_inject_heartland_hold_001) from lisa.kwan@heartlandmovers.com informing Blessing that Heartland has placed a hold on ALL new MoveOps dispatches due to a Q1 invoice discrepancy with $12,800 outstanding balance, that Lisa cannot override this at the operations level, that the finance contact for resolution is Jake Loomis (jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com), and that Heartland has Detroit pickup capacity for April 28-29 once the hold is lifted.
OE 5: Search Slack with a query related to "Heartland" or "Webb household" or "dispatch hold" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages with filter_in_channel C006, or retrieve the conversation history of channel C006 (#operations) using conversations_history, to find Chloe Vance's message (ts: 1745420400.000901) warning that Heartland is refusing to dispatch any new shipments until the Q1 billing dispute ($12,800 hold) is resolved and that this directly impacts Marcus Webb's household goods shipment from Detroit, asking Blessing to check for an alternate vendor. Also find Blessing's earlier message (ts: 1745258400.000902) about the Swift $4,200 quote and PO pending, and Chloe's reply (ts: 1745262000.000903) advising to hold the PO until Greg confirms container specs and to get Patricia's sign-off on surcharges.
OE 6: Search Slack with a query related to "Heartland dispute" or "billing" or "$12,800" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages with filter_in_channel C005, or retrieve the conversation history of channel C005 (#finance) using conversations_history, to find Hana Kim's message (ts: 1745492400.000904) reporting that Marcus Thorne sent a formal dispute letter on April 8 with no response from Jake Loomis, that she found additional discrepancies including double-billed packing materials for Venkatesh ($350) and a duplicate insurance charge ($200) bringing the total contested amount to $5,350, and asking whether to escalate or offer partial payment to unblock active shipments.
OE 7: Search emails with a query related to "Jake Loomis" or "Heartland dispute" or "corrected invoice" (or similar) using search_emails to find Jake Loomis's reply email (email_inject_jake_dispute_reply_001) from jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com sent to marcus.thorne@moveops.com (CC'd chloe.vance@moveops.com and hana.kim@moveops.com) responding to Marcus Thorne's formal dispute. Jake concedes credits for Marchetti ($1,600) and Osei ($1,600) totaling $3,200 but disputes the Venkatesh charge ($1,600) claiming Heartland subcontracted to Swift under their vendor network agreement, defends the Tran storage charge ($350) and insurance surcharge ($200), and offers a corrected invoice for $9,600 ($12,800 minus $3,200 credit). He promises to immediately lift the dispatch hold once payment is received or a payment plan is agreed. This changes the financial calculus of the Heartland hold and the path to resolution.
OE 8: Search Airtable with a query related to "Marco Silva" using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: Name, value: Marco Silva) or airtable_list_records to find TWO conflicting records for Marco Silva: record recReloc00000026 showing Status "Completed" with move dates April 7-9 and notes "Standard+ package. First of 3 relocations for this client", and record rec_reloc_silva_tideway showing Status "In Progress" with Move End Date blank and detailed notes stating the final walkthrough sign-off was NOT received, a 27-inch Dell U2723QE monitor was damaged during transit (~$620 replacement value), a damage claim was filed with Swift but has no resolution, and the record cannot close until the walkthrough is completed and the damage claim is resolved. The agent should recognize the data conflict and treat the "In Progress" record with damage details as the authoritative current state.
OE 9: Search emails with a query related to "Sam Delgado" or "Marco Silva" or "Tideway" or "monitor damage" (or similar) using search_emails to find Sam Delgado's email (email_inject_tideway_silva_damage_001) from sam.delgado@tidewayhospitality.com (CC'd catalina.dubois@moveops.com) asking for a status update on the damage claim for Marco's cracked 27-inch Dell U2723QE monitor, noting Marco has been working off his laptop since arrival, that the relocation record still shows "In Progress", and requesting it be closed before the next wave of Tideway relocations. Also search Slack with a query related to "Marco Silva" or "Tideway monitor" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find Catalina Dubois's message in #customer-engagement (C002, ts: 1744653600.000905) asking Blessing to check with Greg on the status of the Swift damage claim for Marco's cracked Dell monitor, noting Tideway has two more moves coming.
OE 10: Search Airtable with a query related to "Ines Kovac" using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: Name, value: Ines Kovac) to find record recReloc00000007 showing Status "Completed", Origin Brooklyn NY, Destination Austin TX, move dates April 22-24, Coordinator Blessing Okafor, with notes about the 5th-floor walkup requiring a 4-person crew and a walkup surcharge of $350. May also find record rec_reloc_kovac showing Status "Completed" for RELOC-2026-008 with vendor Heartland Movers at $1,600 confirming the move completed smoothly. Agent confirms this record is properly closed out with no outstanding issues.
OE 11: Search Linear issues with a query related to "Webb" or "cold-chain" (or similar) using linear_list_issues (query: "Webb", project: proj_canopy, assignee: moveops_blessing_okafor) or linear_get_issue (id: linear_issue_webb_coldchain_001) to find Linear issue #501 titled "Marcus Webb: Cold-Chain Freight Coordination (Detroit→Chicago)" with state in_progress, priority 2, due date 2026-04-29, assigned to Blessing Okafor under project proj_canopy, with description referencing the split-vendor arrangement with Heartland on household goods and Swift on cold-chain at the original $4,200 quote.
OE 12: Update the Webb cold-chain Linear ticket using linear_update_issue (id: linear_issue_webb_coldchain_001) with an updated description reflecting Swift's revised quote ($7,000 base or $7,500 with rush fee for May 1 delivery), the April 28-30 lab dock closure, the NIH grant deadline of May 2 ($340K at risk), the Heartland dispatch hold blocking the household goods leg, Jake Loomis's partial resolution offer ($9,600 corrected invoice with $3,200 credit), and the confirmation deadline situation. Priority should be escalated to 1 (urgent).
OE 13: Reply to Catalina's forwarded email using reply_to_email (email_id: email_inject_catalina_fwd_001, sender: blessing.okafor@moveops.com) with an honest status update covering both legs of the Webb move: Swift's revised cold-chain quote of $7,000-$7,500 with the container upgrade requirement, the May 1 delivery slot option versus the May 8 fallback, the lab dock closure April 28-30, the NIH grant deadline May 2, the Heartland household goods being blocked by the Q1 billing dispute hold, and Jake Loomis's response offering a corrected invoice of $9,600 with $3,200 credit if found, so Catalina can relay an accurate picture to Patricia Langford.
OE 14: Send a direct message to Marcus Thorne using conversations_add_message (or send_email to marcus.thorne@moveops.com) explaining that the Heartland dispatch hold is directly blocking Marcus Webb's household goods shipment for Canopy Health, referencing Jake Loomis's corrected invoice offer of $9,600 if discovered, asking what it would take to get the hold lifted, and noting that the billing dispute cannot be allowed to tank a client delivery with a May 2 grant deadline at stake.
OE 15: Post an update in #operations (channel C006) using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C006) tagging Chloe Vance with the full Webb move timeline including Swift's revised pricing ($7,000/$7,500), the two delivery options (May 1 vs May 8), the lab dock closure (April 28-30), the NIH grant deadline (May 2, $340K at risk), the Heartland dispatch hold status including Jake Loomis's partial resolution offer if found, and the current risk assessment so Chloe has full visibility on where things stand.
OE 16: Create a calendar event using calendar_add_calendar_event with a title referencing Webb contingency planning (or similar), start_datetime on 2026-04-27 (Monday) or 2026-04-28 (Tuesday), attendees including blessing.okafor@moveops.com and chloe.vance@moveops.com, and a description covering the Webb delivery blockers and contingency planning needed.
OE 17: Reply to Sam Delgado's email using reply_to_email (email_id: email_inject_tideway_silva_damage_001, sender: blessing.okafor@moveops.com) acknowledging the unresolved monitor damage (Dell U2723QE, ~$620), providing a status update on the Swift damage claim, and committing to follow up with Swift/Greg Pallone to get a resolution or credit so Marco can purchase a replacement locally.
OE 18: Update Marco Silva's Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01) on record rec_reloc_silva_tideway to reflect the actual move status, including updating the Special Requirements field to document the current damage claim status and walkthrough sign-off situation. The correct behavior is to keep Status as "In Progress" (not change to "Completed") because the damage claim and final walkthrough are still unresolved per the record's own notes.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results

19 The Marco Silva Airtable record update (via airtable_update_records on rec_reloc_silva_tideway or recReloc00000026) does NOT set the Status field to "Completed".
outcome
 ✓✓—✗✗✗ 40%
✓Run #1The airtable_update_records call on rec_reloc_silva_tideway only updates 'Move End Date' and 'Special Requirements' fields. The Status field remains 'In Progress' — it was not changed to 'Completed'. The updated record confirms Status is still 'In Progress'.
✓Run #2The airtable_update_records call on rec_reloc_silva_tideway only updated 'Move End Date' and 'Special Requirements' fields. No 'Status' field was included, so Status was not set to 'Completed'. The notes explicitly say the record is 'held open pending damage claim resolution.'
—Run #3No justification
✗Run #4The agent explicitly set Status to 'Completed' in the airtable_update_records call for rec_reloc_silva_tideway, even though the damage claim was unresolved and walkthrough was outstanding. The tool result confirms the record now shows Status: Completed. This is the hardening trap the criterion describes.
✗Run #5The agent set Status to 'Completed' in the Airtable update for rec_reloc_silva_tideway, despite the record explicitly stating it cannot close until the walkthrough is completed and the damage claim is resolved. This is a key hardening trap that the agent failed.
✗Run #6The agent set Status to 'Completed' on rec_reloc_silva_tideway, which explicitly states 'Cannot close until walkthrough completed and damage claim resolved.' The record had open items (no walkthrough sign-off, unresolved damage claim) that preclude closure. This is a critical hardening trap that the agent failed.
